workflow.name: Generate continuous PRD updates in Google Docs from Slack, Zoom, Jira, Zendesk, Figma and analytics using OpenAI
workflow.description:
This workflow creates an automated Product Intelligence Engine that continuously collects signals from multiple product sources and generates structured PRD updates using AI.
It ingests conversations, feedback, support tickets, analytics, and design comments, standardizes them, analyzes them with an AI PRD Agent, and automatically updates a Google Doc with structured PRD recommendations. Instead of manually reviewing Slack threads, Zoom calls, Jira comments, support tickets, and customer forms, this workflow centralizes everything into one intelligent PRD analysis system.

**High-Level Architecture** - The workflow runs in 4 layers:

**1. Signal Ingestion Layer**
Captures product signals from:
• Slack (channel messages + app mentions)
• Customer Form submissions
• Zoom recordings (scheduled)
• Jira comments (scheduled)
• Zendesk tickets (scheduled)
• Figma comments (file updates)
• Platform analytics via webhook
• (Extendable to Salesforce / HubSpot)

**2. Standardization Layer**
Each source passes through a Format Node that:
• Extracts relevant text
• Normalizes metadata
• Adds timestamps
• Labels source type
All inputs are converted into a unified "product signal" object.

**3. Intelligence Layer (AI PRD Agent)**
All signals are merged into a single stream using a Merge node.
The PRD Analysis Agent then:
• Extracts feature requests
• Detects scope changes
• Identifies risks and constraints
• Evaluates priority signals
• Detects target user shifts
• Generates structured PRD updates

**4. PRD Governance Layer - output in a Google Doc**
The structured AI output is appended to a Google Doc, which is fully traceable. This creates a living PRD that continuously evolves based on real product signals.

**Required Credentials** (And How To Add Them): You will need to configure the following credentials in n8n:


**1. Slack**
Used for Slack Trigger.
Steps:
Create a Slack App at api.slack.com
Enable:
app_mentions:read
channels:history
chat:write (optional if you want replies)
Install app to workspace
Copy Bot OAuth Token
In n8n → Create Slack API credential
Paste token
Reference - https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=qk5JH6ImK0I

**2. Zoom (OAuth2)**
Used to fetch recordings.
Steps:
Create an OAuth App in Zoom Marketplace
Add the Redirect URL from n8n
Copy Client ID + Secret
Add Zoom OAuth2 credential in n8n
Connect account
Reference - https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=BC6O_3LYgac

**3. Google Docs (OAuth2)**
Used to update PRD document.
Steps:
Create Google Cloud Project
Add Doc URl to n8n
Replace the example Google Doc URL with your own PRD document.
Reference - https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=iieEHvu93dc

**4. Jira (Cloud)**
Steps:
Generate API token from Atlassian
Create Jira Software Cloud credential
Enter:
Email
API token
Domain
Reference - https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=T4z7lzqSZDY

**5. Zendesk**
Steps:
Generate API token
Add Zendesk credential
Enter:
Subdomain
Email
API token

**6. Figma**
Steps:
Generate a personal access token in Figma
Add Figma credentials with the team ID
Paste token

**7. Platform Analytics Webhook**
Replace:
<__PLACEHOLDER_VALUE__your_analytics_api_endpoint__>
With your real analytics endpoint.
You can:
• Send Mixpanel exports
• Send Amplitude exports
• Or POST custom JSON

**What Makes This Powerful**
• Eliminates product signal silos
• Creates AI-driven PRD governance
• Ensures traceability of decisions
• Enables continuous PRD evolution
• Scales across teams

parameters.jsonSchemaExample:
{
	"purpose": "Clear statement of the feature/change purpose",
	"user_problem": "The specific problem users are facing",
	"user_value": "The value this provides to users",
	"target_users": "Who will benefit from this (e.g., heavy podcast listeners, mobile users)",
	"user_feedback": ["feedback quote 1", "feedback quote 2"],
	"assumptions": ["assumption 1", "assumption 2"],
	"out_of_scope": ["item 1 not included", "item 2 not included"],
	"acceptance_criteria": ["criteria 1", "criteria 2"],
	"next_steps": ["action 1", "action 2"]
}

actionFields.text:
={{ '

=== PRD UPDATE ' + $now.toISO() + ' ===

**Purpose:** ' + $json.output.purpose + '

**User Problem:** ' + $json.output.user_problem + '

**User Value:** ' + $json.output.user_value + '

**Target Users:** ' + $json.output.target_users + '

**User Feedback:**
' + $json.output.user_feedback.map(f => '- ' + f).join('
') + '

**Assumptions:**
' + $json.output.assumptions.map(a => '- ' + a).join('
') + '

**Out of Scope:**
' + $json.output.out_of_scope.map(o => '- ' + o).join('
') + '

**Acceptance Criteria:**
' + $json.output.acceptance_criteria.map(c => '- ' + c).join('
') + '

**Next Steps:**
' + $json.output.next_steps.map(s => '- ' + s).join('
') + '

---
' }}

parameters.content:
## How it Works - Layer 1
This layer ingests product signals from multiple sources including Slack, Zoom recordings, Jira, Zendesk, Figma, customer forms, and analytics tools.

## How it Works - Layer 2
This layer standardizes raw inputs from all sources into a unified structure.
The output is a consistent product signal object ready for AI processing.

## How it Works - Layer 3
All formatted data streams are merged into a single unified product intelligence stream.
This step consolidates cross-functional signals into one input object for centralized PRD analysis.

## How it Works - Layer 4
The PRD Agent analyzes aggregated signals using AI to:
Extract feature requests
Detect scope changes
Evaluate priority signals and 
Generate structured PRD update proposals.

## How it Works - Layer 5
All changes are logged in for traceability and governance.

options.systemMessage:
You are a PRD Analysis Agent. Analyze the aggregated product signals from multiple sources (Slack, Zoom, Jira, Figma, Salesforce, HubSpot, Zendesk, customer forms, and analytics). Extract feature requests, detect scope changes, target user changes, identify constraints and risks, evaluate priority signals, and generate structured PRD update proposals.

Provide your analysis in the following structured format:
- purpose: Clear statement of the feature/change purpose
- user_problem: The specific problem users are facing
- user_value: The value this provides to users
- target_users: Who will benefit from this
- user_feedback: Array of relevant user feedback quotes
- assumptions: Key assumptions being made
- out_of_scope: What is explicitly not included
- acceptance_criteria: Array of criteria for completion
- next_steps: Array of recommended next actions

Analyze all signals for:
- Feature requests and their priority
- Scope changes or expansions
- Target user segment changes
- Technical constraints and risks
- Priority signals from multiple sources

parameters.content:
### Zoom Setup
Steps:
1. Create an OAuth App in Zoom Marketplace
2. Add the Redirect URL from n8n
3. Copy Client ID + Secret
4. Add Zoom OAuth2 credential in n8n
Connect account
Reference - https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=BC6O_3LYgac

### Jira Setup
Steps:
1. Generate API token from Atlassian
2. Create Jira Software Cloud credential
3. Enter: Email, API token, Domain
Reference - https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=T4z7lzqSZDY

### Platform Analytics Setup
Steps
1. Replace:
<__PLACEHOLDER_VALUE__your_analytics_api_endpoint__>
With your real analytics endpoint.

### Zendesk Setup
Steps:
1. Generate API token
2. Add Zendesk credential
3. Enter: Subdomain, Email, API token

### Figma Setup
Steps:
1. Generate a personal access token in Figma
2. Add Figma credentials with the team ID
3. Paste token

### Form Setup
Steps 
1. Edit Labels and Input Type to create a feedback form
2. Link can be shared with anyone

### Slack Setup
Steps:
1. Create a Slack App at api.slack.com
Enable:
app_mentions:read
channels:history
chat:write (optional if you want replies)
2. Install app to workspace
3. Copy Bot OAuth Token
4. In n8n → Create Slack API credential
5. Paste token
Reference - https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=qk5JH6ImK0I

### GPT Setup
Update the system message so that it clearly defines the exact PRD structure you expect, including the sections that should be generated.
Replace the free OpenAI API credits with your own API key.

### Google Doc Setup
Steps:
1. Create Google Cloud Project
2. Add Doc URl to n8n
3. Replace the example Google Doc URL with your own PRD document.
Reference - https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=iieEHvu93dc
